heading: DayWeekMonth
text: Specification of recurring periods based on applicable days of week and months.

heading: InstanceOfDayWithinMonth
text: Recurring periods by the nth occurrence of a weekday in a month.

heading: FuzzyTimeCode
text: A code indicating a named time during the day, such as `dawn`, `dusk`, `start of rain`, `start of school`, etc.

heading: SpecialDayTypeCode
text: Code that provides for an identification of special day types.

heading: CalendarWeekWithinMonth
text: Specification of recurring periods in terms of the calendar week(s) of the month.

heading: FuzzyTime
text: An instant in time designated by a named timeReference event (e.g., `dusk`) coupled with a defined offset either before or after the referenced event. The timeReference can be any value from a recognized FuzzyTimeCode. The offset indicates the amount of units of time from this event either before or after the timeReference.

heading: ClockTime
text: A description of a time within a single day (hours, minutes, seconds, etc.), recurring daily. Date components (year, month, day) must not be present.

heading: PublicEventCode
text: Code that provides for an identification of public event types.

heading: TimePeriodOfDay
text: Recurring periods within a single day using start and end times.

heading: WeekCode
text: Code that provides for a unique identification for weeks within a month.

heading: TimeOfDay
text: A description of a time within a single day, which can be an explicit time (e.g., 08:30) or a time reference (e.g., `dusk`).

heading: DateWithinMonth
text: Recurring periods by the nth day in a month.

heading: Period
text: A single valid or invalid period, or a set of repeating periods.

heading: ScheduleThing
text: Added for organizational purposes, to identify classes defined in the Schedule Pattern for ITS.

heading: Schedule
text: The class defining the temporal validity of a situation element or its impact.

heading: OverallPeriod
text: The bounding start and end times of the validity period.

heading: SpecialDay
text: Recurring periods based on special days, including public holidays.

heading: ValidityStatusCode
text: Code for validity status.

heading: PublicHoliday
text: A specialization representing named public holidays.

heading: ScheduleObjectProperty
text: An object property to organize all object properties defined in the ITS Schedule Pattern.

heading: applicableArea
text: Specifies an area associated with a SpecialDay.

heading: applicableDayOfWeek
text: Specifies a day of the week during which the DayWeekMonth object is applicable.

heading: applicableMonth
text: Specifies a month during which the DayWeekMonth object is applicable.

heading: applicableWeek
text: Specifies a week within a month during which the CalendarWeekWithinMonth object is applicable.

heading: dailyEndTime
text: Time of day at which the recurring pattern ends.

heading: dailyStartTime
text: Time of day at which the recurring pattern starts.

heading: exceptionPeriod
text: A period completely or partially within the overall period during which the schedule does not apply. An exceptionPeriod overrules any validPeriod definition.

heading: hasPublicEventType
text: Indication of the type of public event.

heading: hasRecurringDayWeekMonthPeriod
text: A recurring period specified by the intersection of day-of-week, week, and month parameters.

heading: hasRecurringSpecialDay
text: A recurring special day specified by a day type.

heading: hasRecurringTimePeriodOfDay
text: Specification of a applicable time period that starts on applicable days. If the time period spans midnight (i.e. the end time is earlier than the start time), the applicability of the period will extend to the end time on the following day, even if that day is not normally applicable.

heading: hasSpecialDayType
text: The type of special day.

heading: hasValidityStatus
text: Specifies the validity status of the schedule's logic.

heading: validityTimeSpecification
text: The overall container of rules that define the periods when the scheduling logic evaluates to true.

heading: validPeriod
text: A period completely or partially within the overall period during which the schedule applies. A validPeriod can be overruled by an exceptionPeriod and the overall start and end of the overall period.

heading: TimeObjectProperty
text: An object property to organize all object properties defined in the Time Ontology.
